skill.name: observability-engineer
skill.description: Design production monitoring, logging, tracing, SLO, dashboard, and incident response practices for reliable cloud and enterprise systems.
skill.summary: Build observability strategies with SLOs, dashboards, alerts, tracing, log management, and incident response workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 18 potential issues, but review found all were false positives caused by observability, identity, and compliance terminology. SKILL.md contains guidance text only, with no scripts, executable commands, network calls, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts. The skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Windows SAM Database
low_findings.description: The detected text is 'SAML' in an identity integration context, not the Windows SAM database. No credential store access or extraction behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 164 discusses Active Directory and SAML integration for monitoring access. This is normal enterprise authentication terminology, not sensitive database access.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: C2 Keywords
low_findings.description: The detected C2-like tokens are parts of SOC2 compliance references. They describe audit requirements and automated evidence collection, not command and control behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each location uses SOC2 alongside PCI, HIPAA, or compliance monitoring. No beaconing, remote control, payload delivery, or external endpoint is described.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The listed lines describe monitoring, dashboard, architecture, and serverless observability topics. No cryptographic algorithm, implementation, downgrade, or unsafe hash usage appears.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain non-executable observability prose. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic algorithm use.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The detected lines advise avoiding secret logging and describe multi-cloud monitoring. They do not instruct host enumeration, privilege discovery, or stealthy reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 33 is defensive safety guidance, and line 203 is architecture terminology. No command execution or host data collection procedure is present.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The detected line references enterprise identity integration for monitoring access. It does not describe scanning, probing, port enumeration, or target discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is authentication and access management for observability platforms. No network reconnaissance workflow or tooling is included.

content.value_statement: Production systems fail when teams lack clear telemetry, SLOs, and response paths. This skill helps design monitoring, logging, tracing, dashboards, alerts, and incident workflows.
actual_capabilities: Defines SLIs, SLOs, error budgets, and burn-rate alerting strategies.
Plans dashboards, alert routes, escalation workflows, and post-incident review practices.
Designs metrics, logs, traces, and OpenTelemetry collection pipelines.
Recommends observability architectures for Kubernetes, cloud, serverless, and enterprise systems.
Provides monitoring cost, retention, sampling, and vendor evaluation guidance.
Connects technical telemetry with reliability, compliance, and business impact reporting.
limitations: It does not include executable deployment scripts or automation files.
It needs real service details, traffic patterns, and incident history for precise recommendations.
It cannot directly query metrics, logs, traces, or vendor APIs without user-provided access.
It provides design guidance, not guaranteed compliance certification.

use_cases.title: Design SLO Programs
use_cases.description: Create practical SLIs, SLOs, alerts, and error budget workflows for user-facing services.

use_cases.title: Modernize Telemetry Pipelines
use_cases.description: Plan OpenTelemetry, metrics, logs, traces, retention, and vendor-neutral export paths.

use_cases.title: Improve Incident Readiness
use_cases.description: Build dashboards, escalation policies, runbooks, and post-incident review processes.

prompt_templates.title: Review Monitoring Coverage
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner assessment of an existing service
prompt_templates.prompt: Review our monitoring coverage for this service. Identify missing metrics, useful dashboards, alert gaps, and simple next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Create SLOs and Alerts
prompt_templates.prompt: Design SLIs, SLOs, error budgets, and burn-rate alerts for an API with this traffic, latency target, and availability goal.

prompt_templates.title: Plan an Observability Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an observability architecture for this Kubernetes and cloud environment, including metrics, logs, traces, retention, dashboards, and alert routing.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Enterprise Observability
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this enterprise observability strategy for cost, data retention, compliance evidence, incident response, signal quality, and long-term scalability.

output_examples.input: Design observability for a microservices checkout platform.
output: A service map grouped by customer journey and dependency risk.
Recommended golden signals, trace spans, log fields, dashboards, and alerts.
An SLO and incident response plan tied to checkout success rate.

output_examples.input: Reduce alert noise for a 24/7 operations team.
output: A prioritized alert review with noisy, duplicate, and unactionable alerts identified.
A burn-rate alerting model with escalation rules and ownership.
Runbook improvements that clarify diagnosis and response steps.

output_examples.input: Control log storage cost without losing important evidence.
output: A retention model for hot, warm, and archived telemetry data.
Sampling and enrichment guidance for high-volume services.
A vendor-neutral cost review with operational tradeoffs.

best_practices: Start with user journeys, reliability targets, and business impact before choosing tools.
Use actionable alerts with clear ownership, runbooks, and escalation paths.
Review telemetry cost, retention, sampling, and sensitive data handling early.
anti_patterns: Do not collect every metric without ownership, retention, or alerting purpose.
Do not alert on symptoms that no responder can diagnose or fix.
Do not log secrets, personal data, or sensitive payloads into shared systems.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps design monitoring, logging, tracing, dashboards, alerts, SLOs, and incident response workflows.

faq.question: Does it deploy observability tools automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides architecture and operational guidance, but it does not include executable deployment automation.

faq.question: Can it work with OpenTelemetry?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes OpenTelemetry collection, instrumentation, export, sampling, and vendor-neutral pipeline guidance.

faq.question: Is it useful for compliance monitoring?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help plan evidence collection, audit trails, retention, and monitoring controls for regulated environments.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide service architecture, traffic patterns, reliability targets, current telemetry, incident history, vendors, and operational constraints.

faq.question: Can it replace an SRE team?
faq.answer: No. It supports planning and review, but humans must validate production risks and operate the systems.
